mnemonics: Conditional Clauses: IF your condition is TRUE, THEN act, OR ELSE IF another, OR ELSE do this.
Think of conditionals as a fork in the road: the condition evaluates which path (block of code) to take.
keyPoints: A condition is any expression that can be evaluated as True or False (boolean).
Use comparison operators (==, !=, <, >, <=, >=) to form the core of conditions.
Use logical operators (and, or, not) to combine simple conditions into more complex ones.
commonMistakes: Using a single equals sign (=) for comparison instead of a double equals sign (==). The first is for assignment, the second for comparison.
Forgetting the colon (:) at the end of the if, elif, or else statement.
Incorrect indentation of the code blocks that run for each condition, which changes the program's logic.
